title: Topological Quantum Computing

theory.introduction: Topological Quantum Computing (TQC) is an approach to quantum computation that encodes information in topological states of matter. Instead of storing quantum information in fragile qubit states directly, TQC uses global properties of quantum systems that are resistant to local noise. This makes topological systems naturally robust against decoherence, offering a promising path toward fault-tolerant quantum computation.
key_points: Information stored in topological properties of quantum states
Topological states are resistant to local noise

topological_protection.description: Topological systems encode quantum information in global features of quantum states rather than local properties. Small disturbances do not easily change these global features, providing natural protection against noise.

anyons.description: Anyons are quasiparticles that exist in two-dimensional systems. Unlike fermions or bosons, exchanging anyons changes the quantum state in a way that depends on the path taken.

non_abelian_anyons.description: exchange transforms quantum state using unitary operation

mathematical_foundation.topological_invariance: quantum information depends on path topology not exact trajectory
mathematical_foundation.description: Braiding anyons produces unitary transformations acting as quantum gates. The result depends on how particles are braided, not on exact physical movement.

braiding_computation.description: Computation in TQC is performed by moving anyons around each other. The braiding sequence determines quantum gate operations.

topological_qubit.description: Qubits encoded using collective state of anyons

braiding.description: Moving anyons around each other to perform computation

fusion.description: Combining anyons to measure quantum state

topological_phase_of_matter.description: Exotic phases of matter supporting anyons

open_questions: Can non-abelian anyons be reliably created in laboratory?
Which materials best support topological qubits?
How scalable are topological quantum computers?
Can topological qubits outperform superconducting qubits?
What are the best methods to braid anyons precisely?
How can topological systems integrate with existing quantum hardware?
summary: Topological Quantum Computing encodes quantum information in topological states of matter, making it naturally resistant to noise. By using anyons and braiding operations, TQC provides a promising approach toward scalable fault-tolerant quantum computation.
